Frequency Hopping Spread Spectrum

# Frequency Hopping Spread Spectrum

### Hedy Lamarr and George Antheil

[](https://substack.com/@sadashree)

![Ratnakar Sadasyula's avatar](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!R73I!,w_36,h_36,c_fill,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fsubstack-post-media.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2F81a2c542-8b3f-4ade-98d2-a40950cffbe3_2640x1980.jpeg)

[Ratnakar Sadasyula](https://substack.com/@sadashree)

Aug 11, 2021

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

1

Share

Frequency Hopping Spread Spectrum or FHSS, is a method by which radio signals can be transmitted by rapidly switching a carrier among many frequency channels, using a pseudo random sequence, and work on it had been going on for some time. The signals rapidly hop in a predetermined order among the sub frequencies in a frequency band, and this ensures that any interference from other signals would be only for a very short interval. Marconi was the first to experiment with this in 1899.

Tesla was one of the first to patent a frequency hopping method, which he actually called Method of Signalling, describing a system that would enable radio communication without an intercepts or disturbances. Germany made limited use of this frequency hopping technology during World War I, to prevent eavesdropping by the British, who did not yet have the tech to follow this sequence. In the meantime a Polish inventor Leonard Danilecwiz came up with a patent to improve on this.

However the most important invention in frequency hopping technology came not from scientists, but by a Hollywood actress cum amateur inventor, Heddy Lammar and an avant garde music composer George Antheil, who happened to be her friend too.

[](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!Q4Wq!,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fbucketeer-e05bbc84-baa3-437e-9518-adb32be77984.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2F223e40d2-f372-4c71-bc08-3cd977dcd6ab_220x290.jpeg)

![](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!Q4Wq!,w_1456,c_limit,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fbucketeer-e05bbc84-baa3-437e-9518-adb32be77984.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2F223e40d2-f372-4c71-bc08-3cd977dcd6ab_220x290.jpeg)

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

[](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!qsVh!,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fbucketeer-e05bbc84-baa3-437e-9518-adb32be77984.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2Fd979a1df-5b10-4089-bf3b-f286a8530ee2_180x263.jpeg)

![](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!qsVh!,w_1456,c_limit,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fbucketeer-e05bbc84-baa3-437e-9518-adb32be77984.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2Fd979a1df-5b10-4089-bf3b-f286a8530ee2_180x263.jpeg)

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

Heddy Lamarr's real name was Hedwig Eva Maria Kiesler, a Jew from Vienna, who fled after the Nazis occupied Austria, along with her mother Getrude. Arriving in the US she gave her race as Hebrew , she was just around 11 then. Her first husband Friedrich Mandl was the 3rd richest man in Austria, a well known arms dealer, with close ties to the Fascist Govt in Italy, and was twice her age, when they got married. However his rather dominating nature, ensured she fled a failed marriage.

Anyway she rose to be one of Hollywood's leading heroines, she got her screen name from Louis Mayer, MGM's boss then, more often cast as a sultry seductress. One of her best known movies was the bibilical Epic Samson and Delillah. She had no formal training in science, she was more or less self taught, and when not acting she spent her time on various inventions that included an improved traffic spotlight, and a tablet that would dissolve in water to create a carbonated drink.

Howard Hughes was one of the few who knew about Lamarrs inventiveness, and they even had a brief relationship for some time. He even put his entire team of science engineers at her disposal saying they would make anything she asked for. When World War II broke out, Heddy as not really comfortable sitting in Hollywood and making money. She was willing to quit MGM, and go to the newly established Inventor's Council, in Washington DC.

She came to understand that, radio controlled torpedoes, could be jammed or easily set off course by the enemy. And it was this that motivated her to come up with a Frequency Hopping signal, that coud not be tracked, as she contacted her friend George Antheill, a leading composer.

The Radio controlled torpedoes were one of the most lethal weapons in WWII, which could severely damage or sink several ships at one go. However these torpedoes could be easily detected and jammed, leading them off course. Much like Heddy, he was a multi talented person, with a wide variety of interests. A celebrated avant garde musician, who also composed BGM for many Hollywood movies. He was also a good writer, novelist and had a fairly good knowledge of communication networks.

Heddy had a fairly good knowledge of torpedoes from her ex husband, who was one of the leading arms manufacturer., and this led her to develop the idea of frequency hopping to avoid radio detection in conjunction with her friend George Antheil. What they did was to use a piano roll composed by Antheil, to randomly change the signal sent between control center and torpedo at short bursts within a range of 88 frequenices, that was the number of black and white keys on a piano board.

Only the controlling ship and the torpedo would be knowing the specific code for the frequency sequence. And for the enemy, it was impossible to jam and scan all the 88 frequencies, as that wud need a lot of resources. Antheil controlled the sequence using a player piano.

However the US Navy did not really accept Heddy Lamar and George Antheil's patent for Frequency Hopping, first because it was not yet technically feasible to do so, and next, they were opposed to inventions coming from outside the military.

However during the 1962 Cuban missile crisis, the Navy implemented the Frequency Hopping idea developed by Heddy and George. And this was after the patent for it had already expired. Earlier during the 1950s some of the private firms developed CDMA using some of the concepts.

Though Heddy Lamarr and George Antheill's frequency hopping spectrum idea was not implemented initially, it would later become the basis for many breakthroughs like Bluetooth, Wi Fi, CDMA in Wireless telephony. In 1997 the duo received the EFF Pioneer Award for their work.

Today Bluetooth, CDMA, Wi Fi have become a part of our life, which we use daily one way or another. And the next time when you do so, remember that the pioneering work in this was done by a Hollywood actress, Heddy Lamarr and a music composer George Antheill.
